stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Started in 1947, the South African Word Wizard Championship is a national spelling competition.
  • The words used in the competition come from many different language families.
  • In 2014, Sipho Dlamini won by correctly spelling the word “fynbos.”
  • “Fynbos” derives from the Afrikaans words for “fine” and “bush.”
  • In 2017, Aaliyah Naidoo won by correctly spelling the word “bandobast.”
  • “Bandobast” derives from the Persian word “band-o-bast.”
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference in the origins of the two words. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Sipho Dlamini won the championship in 2014, and Aaliyah Naidoo won it in 2017.
Both “fynbos” and “bandobast” are words used in the South African Word Wizard Championship.
The South African Word Wizard Championship uses words from many different language families.
“Fynbos” derives from the Afrikaans words for “fine” and “bush,” whereas “bandobast” derives from the Persian word “band-o-bast.”

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a difference in the origins of the two words by stating that “fynbos” derives from Afrikaans, whereas “bandobast” derives from Persian.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence notes the winners and years but doesn’t address the words’ origins. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a similarity (both are used in the championship) rather than a difference in origins. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes the championship in general; it doesn’t emphasize a difference in the origins of the two specific words.
